I need to understand the structure of the unsloth codebase to plan adding an `export_to_onnx` method. Please:

1. Find the top-level directory structure of this project
2. Find all files related to saving, exporting, or serializing models (look for files with names like save.py, export.py, serialization.py, etc.)
3. Find where existing save/export methods are defined (e.g., `save_pretrained`, `save_model`, `push_to_hub`, etc.)
4. Look for any existing ONNX-related code
5. Find files that define the main model classes (look for class definitions that wrap or patch HuggingFace models)
6. Look for how LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) are stored/accessed in the codebase
7. Find any `__init__.py` files that expose public APIs

Please be thorough - read the actual content of key files, not just list them.

input.prompt:
I need to understand how LoRA adapter weights are structured in unsloth to plan an `export_to_onnx` method. Please:

1. Search for references to "lora_A", "lora_B", "lora_alpha", "lora_rank", "r =", "target_modules" in the codebase
2. Find how LoRA layers are implemented (look for LoraLinear, Linear4bit, or similar class names)
3. Find how the model's named_modules() or named_parameters() gives access to LoRA weights
4. Look for any existing code that iterates over LoRA layers and extracts A/B matrices
5. Find how adapter config (rank, alpha, target_modules) is stored and accessed
6. Search for PEFT-related imports and how unsloth interacts with PEFT's LoRA implementation
7. Look for any existing serialization of adapter weights

Read actual file contents for the most relevant files.

72	@functools.cache

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

197	        # pickle_module   = pickle,
198	        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
199	    )
200	    return

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass
304	
305	    if tags is not None:
306	        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
307	        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
308	    else:
309	        tags = ["unsloth",]
310	    pass
311	    save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags
312	
313	    if ((save_method == "lora") or (save_method == "merged_4bit")) and push_to_hub:
314	        if token is None:
315	            raise RuntimeError(
316	                "Unsloth: Pushing to HF requires a token. Pass `token = 'hf_....'`\n"\
317	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens."
318	            )
319	        pass
320	
321	        if save_method == "lora":
322	            print("Unsloth: Saving LoRA adapters. Please wait...")
323	        elif save_method == "merged_4bit":

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass
233

238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

275	if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)

400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass

419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass

469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result

1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",

1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",

1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",

1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",

1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

770	def install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking():
771	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
772	    # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??
773	    # env = { **os.environ, "LLAMA_CUDA": "1", }
774	    # Force make clean
775	    check = os.system("make clean -C llama.cpp")
776	    IS_CMAKE = False
777	    if check == 0:
778	        # Uses old MAKE
779	        n_jobs = max(int(psutil.cpu_count()*1.5), 1)
780	        full_command = ["make", "all", "-j"+str(n_jobs), "-C", "llama.cpp"]
781	        IS_CMAKE = False
782	    else:
783	        # Uses new CMAKE
784	        n_jobs = max(int(psutil.cpu_count()), 1) # Use less CPUs since 1.5x faster
785	        check = os.system("cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON")
786	        if check != 0:
787	            raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp using os.system(...) with error {check}. Please report this ASAP!")
788	        pass
789	        # f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
790	        full_command = [
791	            "cmake", "--build", "llama.cpp/build",
792	            "--config", "Release",
793	            "-j"+str(n_jobs),
794	            "--clean-first",
795	            "--target",
796	        ] + LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS
797	        IS_CMAKE = True
798	    pass
799	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062

261	def _convert_lora_to_linear(module: LoraLayer, adapter_name: str = "default"):
262	    base_layer = module.get_base_layer()
263	    weight = base_layer.weight
264	
265	    assert isinstance(weight, bnb.nn.Params4bit)
266	    quant_state = weight.quant_state
267	    original_dtype = quant_state.dtype
268	
269	    w_dq = dequantize_4bit(weight.data, quant_state).float()
270	    lora_delta = (
271	        module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
272	        @ module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
273	        * module.scaling[adapter_name]
274	    )
275	    w_dq += lora_delta.float()
276	    w_dq = w_dq.to(original_dtype)
277	
278	    new_module = torch.nn.Linear(
279	        w_dq.shape[1], w_dq.shape[0], bias=module.base_layer.bias is not None
280	    )
281	    new_module.weight.data = torch.nn.Parameter(w_dq, requires_grad=False)
282	    if module.lora_bias[adapter_name]:
283	        bias_data = module.base_layer.bias.data + module.lora_B[adapter_name].bias
284	        new_module.bias.data = torch.nn.Parameter(bias_data, requires_grad=False)
285	    return new_module
286	
287	
288	def convert_lora_to_linear(model: torch.nn.Module):
289	    replace_module(model, LoraLayer, _convert_lora_to_linear)
290	    assert not any(isinstance(module, LoraLayer) for module in model.modules())
291	    return model

15	def formatting_prompts_func(examples):
16	    convos = examples["messages"]
17	    texts = [tokenizer.apply_chat_template(convo, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=False) for convo in convos]
18	    return {"text": texts}

20	print(f"\n{'='*80}")

24	if torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
25	    compute_dtype = torch.bfloat16
26	    attn_implementation = 'flash_attention_2'
27	else:
28	    compute_dtype = torch.float16
29	    attn_implementation = 'sdpa'
30	
31	model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
32	    model_name="unsloth/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
33	    max_seq_length=2048,
34	    dtype=compute_dtype,
35	    load_in_4bit=True,
36	    load_in_8bit=False,
37	    full_finetuning=False,
38	    attn_implementation=attn_implementation
39	)
40	
41	tokenizer = get_chat_template(
42	    tokenizer,
43	    chat_template="llama-3.1",
44	)
45	
46	# Load small dataset for quick training
47	dataset_train = load_dataset("allenai/openassistant-guanaco-reformatted", split="train[:100]")
48	dataset_train = dataset_train.map(formatting_prompts_func, batched=True)
49	
50	print("✅ Base model loaded successfully!")
51	
52	print(f"\n{'='*80}")
53	print("🔍 PHASE 2: First Fine-tuning")

56	model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
57	    model,
58	    r=16,
59	    target_modules=['k_proj', 'q_proj', 'v_proj', 'o_proj', "gate_proj", "down_proj", "up_proj"],
60	    lora_alpha=16,
61	    lora_dropout=0,
62	    bias="none",
63	    use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth",
64	    random_state=3407,
65	    use_rslora=False,
66	    loftq_config=None,
67	)
68	
69	from unsloth import is_bfloat16_supported
70	
71	trainer = SFTTrainer(
72	    model=model,
73	    tokenizer=tokenizer,
74	    train_dataset=dataset_train,
75	    dataset_text_field="text",
76	    max_seq_length=2048,
77	    data_collator=DataCollatorForSeq2Seq(tokenizer=tokenizer),
78	    dataset_num_proc=2,
79	    packing=False,
80	    args=TrainingArguments(
81	        per_device_train_batch_size=2,
82	        gradient_accumulation_steps=4,
83	        warmup_ratio=0.1,
84	        max_steps=10,  # Very short training for test
85	        learning_rate=2e-4,
86	        fp16=not is_bfloat16_supported(),
87	        bf16=is_bfloat16_supported(),
88	        logging_steps=5,
89	        optim="adamw_8bit",
90	        lr_scheduler_type="linear",
91	        seed=3407,
92	        output_dir="outputs",
93	        report_to="none",
94	    ),
95	)
96	
97	trainer_stats = trainer.train()

2502	def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
2503	    self,
2504	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2505	    tokenizer            = None,
2506	    torchao_config       = None,
2507	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2508	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2509	):
2510	    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint
2511	
2512	    Args
2513	      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
2515	      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
2516	    """
2517	    # first merge the lora weights
2518	    arguments = dict(locals())
2519	    arguments["model"]        = self
2520	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
2521	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
2522	    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2523	    del arguments["self"]
2524	    del arguments["torchao_config"]
2525	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2526	    for _ in range(3):
2527	        gc.collect()
2528	
2529	    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
2530	    from torchao import quantize_
2531	    if torchao_config is None:
2532	        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
2533	        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
2534	    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)
2535	
2536	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
2537	    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
2538	        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
2539	    else:
2540	        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
2541	    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
2542	        arguments["save_directory"],
2543	        device_map = "auto",
2544	        quantization_config = quantization_config,
2545	        **kwargs,
2546	    )
2547	
2548	    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"
2549	
2550	    if push_to_hub:
2551	        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
2552	        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
2553	        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
2554	        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
2555	    else:
2556	        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
2557	        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
2558	    pass
2559	    for _ in range(3):
2560	        gc.collect()
2561	pass

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Summary: LoRA Adapter Weight Structure in Unsloth

Based on my analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how LoRA adapter weights are structured and accessed:

### 1. **LoRA Layer Structure**

LoRA layers in unsloth are PEFT-based and wrapped in the following classes:
- **`peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit`** (Peft_Linear4bit) - for 4-bit quantized linear layers
- **`peft.tuners.lora.Linear`** (Peft_Linear) - for standard linear layers
- **`bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit`** (Bnb_Linear4bit) - for bitsandbytes 4-bit layers

**Key Files:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` - Contains utility functions `get_lora_parameters()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()` that extract LoRA weights
- `/unsloth/save.py` - Contains `_merge_lora()` function showing how A/B matrices are merged
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Contains the actual LoRA computation logic

### 2. **Accessing LoRA Weights Structure**

Each LoRA-enabled layer has these attributes:

```python
# For a LoRA layer (e.g., layer.self_attn.q_proj):
layer.lora_A[adapter_name]      # Linear module with .weight (shape: [r, input_dim])
layer.lora_B[adapter_name]      # Linear module with .weight (shape: [output_dim, r])
layer.scaling[adapter_name]     # Scalar scaling factor (lora_alpha / r)
layer.base_layer.weight         # Original layer weight
layer.disable_adapters           # Boolean flag
layer.merged                     # Boolean flag
```

**Example from `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-231):**

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
```

### 3. **Adapter Configuration Access**

Adapter configs are stored in `model.peft_config` dictionary (accessed from PEFT):

```python
# Access adapter configuration from file: /unsloth/models/llama.py (lines 2331-2334)
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()

# Key configuration parameters:
peft_config["r"]                    # Rank (e.g., 16, 32, 64)
peft_config["lora_alpha"]           # Alpha scaling factor
peft_config["lora_dropout"]         # Dropout probability
peft_config["target_modules"]       # List of module names to apply LoRA to
peft_config["modules_to_save"]      # Modules to fully train
peft_config["bias"]                 # Bias type: "none", "all", or "lora_only"
peft_config["use_rslora"]           # Rank-stabilized LoRA flag
peft_config["base_model_name_or_path"] # Base model identifier
```

### 4. **Iterating Over LoRA Layers**

**From `/unsloth/models/granite.py` (lines 517-529):**

```python
for name, module in model.named_modules():
    if isinstance(module, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # This module has LoRA adapters
        weight = module.weight
        quant_state = weight.quant_state  # For quantized layers
```

**From `/unsloth/models/gemma.py` (lines 382-386):**

```python
for name, param in model.named_parameters():
    if ".lora_A." in name or ".lora_B." in name:
        param.requires_grad_(True)  # Identify LoRA parameters by name
```

### 5. **LoRA Weight Matrices - Mathematical Structure**

From `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` documentation (lines 224-252):

```
For a linear layer: Output = Input @ W + b

With LoRA: Output = Input @ W + Input @ (A @ B) * scaling + b

Where:
- A has shape [input_dim, r]         (lora_A.weight)
- B has shape [r, output_dim]        (lora_B.weight)
- r is the rank (typically 8-64)
- scaling = lora_alpha / r
```

### 6. **Extracting LoRA Delta Weights**

**From `/tests/utils/hf_utils.py` (lines 270-274):**

```python
# Computing the LoRA delta to merge into base weights
lora_delta = (
    module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
    @ module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
    * module.scaling[adapter_name]
)
# Result shape: [output_dim, input_dim]
w_dq += lora_delta.float()
```

### 7. **Key Data Flow for ONNX Export**

For `export_to_onnx` planning, you'll need:

1. **Iterate over modules:**
   ```python
   for name, module in model.named_modules():
       if isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear, Bnb_Linear4bit)):
           # Extract LoRA info
   ```

2. **Get active adapter:**
   ```python
   active_adapter = module.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(module, "active_adapters") else "default"
   ```

3. **Extract matrices:**
   ```python
   A = module.lora_A[active_adapter].weight  # [input_dim, rank]
   B = module.lora_B[active_adapter].weight  # [rank, output_dim]
   scaling = module.scaling[active_adapter]   # scalar
   ```

4. **Get config:**
   ```python
   adapter_config = model.peft_config["default"]
   rank = adapter_config.r
   alpha = adapter_config.lora_alpha
   target_modules = adapter_config.target_modules
   ```

### 8. **Existing Serialization Patterns**

From `/unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-187), the `_merge_lora()` function shows the standard merge pattern:
- Base weight W is extracted and dequantized if needed
- LoRA delta is computed as: `A.t() @ B.t() * scaling`
- Result merged into base weight
- Both weight and quant_state are managed separately

### 9. **Files Most Relevant for ONNX Export**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Core LoRA weight extraction utilities |
| `/unsloth/save.py` | Weight merging and serialization logic |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | LoRA computation and layer structure |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | Config access and adapter patching |
| `/tests/utils/hf_utils.py` | Example patterns for weight conversion |

This structure should give you everything needed to plan an `export_to_onnx` method that properly captures and exports LoRA adapter weights.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the unsloth codebase. Let me create a detailed summary document.

## Comprehensive Analysis: Unsloth Codebase Structure for `export_to_onnx` Implementation

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here is a detailed report on the structure and key components relevant to adding an `export_to_onnx` method:

---

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main package initialization
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template utilities
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── save.py                     # Core save/export functionality (105KB)
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                  # Trainer patches
├── models/                     # Model implementations
├── kernels/                    # CUDA/Triton optimized kernels
├── dataprep/                   # Data preparation utilities
├── registry/                   # Model registry
└── utils/                      # General utilities
```

**Key Observations:**
- All public APIs are exposed through `__init__.py` (line 261-266)
- The `save.py` module is the central location for all save/export functionality
- Models are wrapped/patched in the `models/` directory

---

### 2. **Files Related to Saving/Exporting/Serialization**

**Primary file: `/unsloth/save.py`** (2600+ lines)

Functions exposed in `__all__`:
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

**Key save-related functions:**
- `unsloth_save_model()` (line 205) - Main save function with multiple methods: lora, merged_16bit, merged_4bit
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321) - Method patched onto models
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 1364) - Hugging Face upload
- `save_to_gguf()` (line 952) - GGUF format export
- `save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276) - Generic GGUF export
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (line 2211) - GGML conversion
- `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (line 2129) - Custom LoRA save
- `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569) - Patches model methods

---

### 3. **Existing Save/Export Methods**

Methods patched onto models via `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2658-2676):

```python
# Standard save methods
model.push_to_hub                  # Original HuggingFace method (wrapped)
model.save_pretrained              # Original HuggingFace method

# Unsloth extensions
model.push_to_hub_merged           # Save merged model to HF
model.save_pretrained_merged       # Save merged model locally
model.push_to_hub_gguf             # Save as GGUF to HF
model.save_pretrained_gguf         # Save as GGUF locally
model.save_pretrained_torchao      # Save with TorchAO quantization
model.push_to_hub_ggml             # Save as GGML to HF
model.save_pretrained_ggml         # Save as GGML locally
```

**Entry point:** `patch_saving_functions()` is called in:
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2188, 2204, 2902)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964)

---

### 4. **ONNX-Related Code**

**Result: No existing ONNX code found in the codebase.**

This means you're adding a completely new feature with no conflicting implementations.

---

### 5. **Main Model Classes**

**Class hierarchy:**

```
Base Models:
├── FastLlamaModel           (/models/llama.py)
├── FastLanguageModel        (/models/loader.py) - inherits from FastLlamaModel
├── FastMistralModel         (/models/mistral.py)
├── FastQwen2Model           (/models/qwen2.py)
├── FastQwen3Model           (/models/qwen3.py)
├── FastQwen3MoeModel        (/models/qwen3_moe.py)
├── FastGraniteModel         (/models/granite.py)
├── FastCohereModel          (/models/cohere.py)
├── FastGemmaModel           (/models/gemma.py)
├── FastGemma2Model          (/models/gemma2.py)
└── FastFalconH1Model        (/models/falcon_h1.py)

Vision Models:
├── FastVisionModel          (/models/loader.py)
└── FastTextModel            (/models/loader.py)

Generic Models:
├── FastModel                (/models/loader.py)
└── PatchFastRL              (/models/rl.py)
```

**Key trait:** These are wrapper classes that:
- Load base transformers models
- Apply PEFT (LoRA) adapters
- Patch forward passes for optimization
- Methods are dynamically bound via `types.MethodType()`

Example from `/unsloth/models/llama.py`:
- `FastLlamaModel.from_pretrained()` - loads and patches model
- `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` - applies LoRA
- Returns model with patched methods

---

### 6. **LoRA Adapter Weights Access and Storage**

**Key function:** `get_lora_parameters_bias()` in `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

```python
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,                               # Base weight matrix
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None), # Quantization state (for 4-bit)
        proj.lora_A[adapter].weight,     # LoRA A matrix
        proj.lora_B[adapter].weight,     # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],           # Scaling factor
        base_layer.bias,                 # Bias
    )
```

**LoRA weight computation** (from `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`):
- LoRA modifies layers: `W_new = W_original + (A @ B) * scaling`
- A matrix: low-rank weight matrices (stored as `lora_A[adapter].weight`)
- B matrix: low-rank weight matrices (stored as `lora_B[adapter].weight`)
- Scaling: alpha/rank scaling factor (stored as `scaling[adapter]`)

**Important LoRA layers:**
Located via PEFT framework - typically applied to:
- Attention layers: `q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj`
- MLP layers: `gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj`

Access pattern:
```python
for layer in model.modules():
    if hasattr(layer, 'lora_A'):
        # layer.lora_A['default'].weight  # LoRA A matrix
        # layer.lora_B['default'].weight  # LoRA B matrix
        # layer.scaling['default']         # Scaling factor
```

---

### 7. **Public API Exposure via __init__.py**

**File:** `/unsloth/__init__.py`

Current public exports (lines 261-266):
```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *
```

**What's exposed from save.py:**
- `print_quantization_methods`
- `unsloth_save_model`
- `save_to_gguf`
- `patch_saving_functions`
- `create_huggingface_repo`

**Model loaders exposed:**
- `FastLanguageModel`
- `FastVisionModel`
- `FastTextModel`
- `FastModel`

---

### 8. **Integration Points for `export_to_onnx`**

Based on the codebase structure, here are the key integration points:

**A. Where to add the function:**
- Primary location: `/unsloth/save.py` (follows existing export pattern)
- Name suggestion: `unsloth_export_to_onnx()` or `export_to_onnx()`

**B. Signature pattern (following existing conventions):**

```python
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    opset_version: int = 18,
    do_constant_folding: bool = True,
    use_external_data_format: bool = False,
    optimize_model: bool = True,
    # ... other ONNX-specific parameters
):
    """Export Unsloth model to ONNX format."""
    # Implementation
```

**C. How to patch it onto models:**

Add to `patch_saving_functions()` around line 2661-2667:
```python
model.save_pretrained_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
model.push_to_hub_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_onnx_to_hub, model)
```

**D. Key challenges to handle:**

1. **Quantized models (4-bit):** Need to dequantize using `fast_dequantize()` from `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`
   - Check for `quant_state` attribute on weights
   - Use `fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)` to convert to float32

2. **LoRA merging:** Before ONNX export, decide:
   - Export with LoRA merged: Use `_merge_lora()` function (line 159 in save.py)
   - Or export LoRA adapters separately

3. **Dynamic shapes:** ONNX requires explicit shape definition
   - Input: `(batch_size, sequence_length, hidden_dim)`
   - Consider dynamic vs static shapes

4. **Operator compatibility:** Some Unsloth optimizations (custom kernels) need standard torch equivalents
   - Use standard PyTorch ops for ONNX export

---

### 9. **Key Dependencies and Utilities**

**Important imports to use:**

From `save.py`:
```python
from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
```

From `kernels/utils.py`:
```python
fast_dequantize()      # Convert 4-bit quantized weights to float
get_lora_parameters_bias()  # Extract A, B, scaling from LoRA layers
```

From HuggingFace utilities:
```python
from huggingface_hub import HfApi, get_token
```

---

### 10. **Test Structure for Reference**

**Test location:** `/tests/saving/`

Directory structure:
```
tests/saving/
├── test_unsloth_save.py          # Main save tests
├── language_models/              # Language model tests
│   ├── test_merge_*.py
│   ├── test_push_to_hub_*.py
│   └── test_merged_model_perplexity_*.py
├── vision_models/                # Vision model tests
├── text_to_speech_models/        # TTS model tests
├── gpt-oss-merge/               # Specialized tests
└── non_peft/                     # Non-LoRA tests
```

**Test pattern for new export method:**
```python
@pytest.fixture(scope="session", params=model_to_test)
def loaded_model_tokenizer(request):
    # Load model with LoRA
    # Apply LoRA adapters
    return model, tokenizer

def test_export_to_onnx(loaded_model_tokenizer):
    model, tokenizer = loaded_model_tokenizer
    # Test export functionality
```

---

### 11. **Import Order Constraints**

**Critical note from `__init__.py` (lines 25-42):**

Unsloth must be imported before `transformers`, `peft`, and `trl` to apply optimizations. This is important for your ONNX export feature - ensure it works correctly regardless of import order.

---

### 12. **File Paths Summary**

**Core files for ONNX implementation:**

| File | Purpose | Lines |
|------|---------|-------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | Where to add export function | 2600+ |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Get LoRA parameters, dequantize | Key utilities |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Understand LoRA merging | Reference |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | Model loading patterns | 1000+ |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | Specific model implementation | 2900+ |
| `/unsloth/__init__.py` | Public API exposure | 270 |
| `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Test examples | 200+ |

---

### 13. **Recommended Implementation Strategy**

1. **Create function in `save.py`:**
   - Name: `unsloth_export_to_onnx()`
   - Handle both merged and LoRA-only exports
   - Support quantized (4-bit) models

2. **Add wrapper method:**
   - Name: `export_to_onnx()` (for cleaner API)
   - Follow pattern of `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()`

3. **Patch onto models:**
   - Add to `patch_saving_functions()` around line 2661
   - Use `types.MethodType()` for binding

4. **Export to `__all__` in save.py:**
   - Add "unsloth_export_to_onnx" to line 48-54

5. **Add tests:**
   - Create `/tests/saving/test_export_onnx.py`
   - Test with multiple model types (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma)
   - Test both 4-bit and 16-bit models

---

This comprehensive analysis provides the foundation for implementing `export_to_onnx` following the established Unsloth patterns and conventions.

## Comprehensive Analysis of Unsloth's Save/Export Method Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's how unsloth exposes its save and export methods:

### 1. **Core Export Functions in `/unsloth/save.py`**

The save module (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495291939-ior2dt/unsloth/save.py`) defines these key wrapper functions that get attached to models:

#### Merged/Standard Save Methods:
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321) - Wrapper for saving with merged LoRA weights
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 1364) - Wrapper for pushing merged models to HuggingFace Hub
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 2413) - Generic version for vision/other models
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (line 2456) - Generic push_to_hub for vision/other models

#### GGUF Quantization Methods:
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768) - Converts to GGUF format and saves locally
- `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` (line 1951) - Converts to GGUF format and pushes to Hub
- `save_to_gguf_generic()` (line 2276) - Generic GGUF conversion for vision models

#### GGML (LoRA-specific) Methods:
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (line 2143) - Converts LoRA to GGML format and pushes to Hub
- `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (line 2211) - Converts LoRA to GGML format and saves locally

#### TorchAO Quantization:
- `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502) - Quantizes with torchao and saves

#### Core Patching Mechanism:
- `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569) - **KEY FUNCTION** that monkey-patches models with all save/export methods

### 2. **How Methods Are Attached to Models**

The `patch_saving_functions()` function (lines 2569-2678) is the central orchestrator:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # ... wraps original push_to_hub with unsloth_push_to_hub ...
    
    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    
    return model
```

**Key techniques used:**
- Uses Python's `types.MethodType()` to dynamically bind functions as instance methods
- Differentiates between language models and vision models (vision models have fewer options)
- Only attaches methods if model has a `config` attribute

### 3. **When Patching Occurs**

The patching happens in `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (and similar model files):

**File:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py`
- Line 2188: `patch_saving_functions(tokenizer)` - patches tokenizer methods
- Line 2204: `patch_saving_functions(model)` - patches model methods
- Line 2902: Another patching location for certain model types

Also in vision models (`/unsloth/models/vision.py`):
- Line 702-703: Patches both model and tokenizer with `vision=True` flag
- Line 918, 964: Additional patching locations

### 4. **Export/Re-export Mechanism**

**File:** `/unsloth/__init__.py` (lines 261-266)
```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *                  # <-- Exports all from save.py
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *
```

**File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (lines 48-54)
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

Note: The individual method wrapper functions are NOT in `__all__`, but they're still used internally and attached to models via `patch_saving_functions()`.

### 5. **How Methods Work - Delegation Pattern**

All wrapper methods follow a consistent pattern:

1. Collect arguments via `dict(locals())`
2. Replace model reference: `arguments["model"] = self`
3. Delete `self` from arguments
4. Delegate to lower-level internal function (usually `unsloth_save_model()` or `unsloth_generic_save()`)

Example from `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 1321-1361):
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(self, save_directory, tokenizer=None, save_method="merged_16bit", ...):
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)  # Delegates to core function
```

### 6. **Summary of Available Methods on Models**

After `patch_saving_functions()` is called, models have these methods:

**Language Models:**
- `.save_pretrained_merged()`
- `.push_to_hub_merged()`
- `.save_pretrained_gguf()`
- `.push_to_hub_gguf()`
- `.save_pretrained_ggml()`
- `.push_to_hub_ggml()`
- `.save_pretrained_torchao()`

**Vision Models:**
- `.save_pretrained_merged()`
- `.push_to_hub_merged()`
- `.save_pretrained_gguf()` (mapped to `save_to_gguf_generic`)
- `.push_to_hub_gguf()` (mapped to `save_to_gguf_generic`)
- `.save_pretrained_torchao()`

Plus the patched `.push_to_hub()` method is enhanced to add Unsloth tags/metadata.

### 7. **How to Add `export_to_onnx` Method**

Based on this architecture, here's the pattern you'd follow to add an `export_to_onnx` method:

1. **Create the wrapper function** in `/unsloth/save.py`:
```python
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    output_path: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    opset_version: int = 14,
    optimize_model: bool = True,
    ...
):
    """Export model to ONNX format"""
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)  # or create unsloth_onnx_save()
    # ... ONNX-specific conversion code ...
```

2. **Attach it in `patch_saving_functions()`** (around line 2665):
```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

3. **Add to `__all__`** if you want direct import:
```python
__all__ = [
    # ... existing ...
    "unsloth_export_to_onnx",  # or just keep it internal
]
```

### 8. **Key Files for Reference**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | All save/export function implementations (2679 lines) |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | Where `patch_saving_functions()` is called for LLaMA models |
| `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | Where vision model patching happens |
| `/unsloth/__init__.py` | Top-level imports and exports |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | Contains `FastLanguageModel` class (lines 107+) |
| `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Test file showing usage of all save methods |

This architecture makes it easy to add new export/save formats because they all follow the same delegation pattern and get attached via the same `types.MethodType()` mechanism.

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass
